title: hieracium prenanthopsis
side_effects: Hieracium prenanthopsis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Prolonged consumption may lead to liver damage and kidney failure. Additionally, improper use of the plant may cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals. In severe cases, Hieracium prenanthopsis can interact with medications, exacerbating conditions such as high blood pressure and diabetes.
